description: Neutral mental health topics that resonate across all audiences. Used alongside men's keywords at 30% rate for Phase 1 audience expansion.

topics.angle: You look fine on the outside. Inside you're sprinting just to stand still.
Going through the motions isn't the same as being okay. Here's how to tell.
Complex trauma rewires you slowly. Recovery happens the same way — small, quiet shifts.
You're not broken. Your body learned to stay alert. It can learn to stand down.
Going from 0 to 100 isn't a character flaw. It's an unregulated nervous system asking for help.
AI won't replace therapy. But at 3am when nobody's awake, something honest is better than nothing.
Therapy isn't the only path. Here's what actually works when traditional therapy isn't an option.
Sobriety doesn't fix your mental health. It removes the thing that was numbing it.
The hardest part of getting sober isn't the drink. It's everything that came with it.
Bad sleep doesn't just make you tired. It rewires your brain to be more anxious by tomorrow.
Can't sleep because you're anxious. Anxious because you can't sleep. Here's how to break it.
Winter doesn't make you weak. Your brain genuinely runs differently with less light. That's all.
Money stress doesn't just live in your bank account. It lives in your shoulders, your sleep, your patience.
Burnout isn't a productivity problem. It's a sign you've been giving from an empty cup for too long.
You can't think your way out of burnout. Recovery is slower, quieter, more boring than you want it to be.
You can be surrounded by people and still feel like a ghost. That's not your fault — it's the design of modern life.
Working from home gave you flexibility. It also quietly took away the small human contact that kept you sane.
You're not weak for feeling worse after scrolling. The apps are literally designed to keep you slightly miserable.
You don't need a 30-day cleanse. You need an hour where your phone isn't an extension of your hand.

topics.keyword: grief that doesn't fit a timeline
topics.angle: Grief isn't linear. The waves come back. That's not regression — that's love still doing its work.
Chronic illness isn't just a medical condition. It's a daily negotiation between who you are and what your body allows.
3am panic attacks aren't random. Your nervous system has been waiting all day for the quiet to finally process.
Medication isn't a moral failure. Neither is choosing not to take it. Here's what works either way.
If meditation makes you more anxious, you're not failing at it. You just need a different door in.
You don't need to write beautifully. You just need to put the noise on paper so it stops echoing in your head.
You don't need a gym membership. A 20-minute walk genuinely outperforms most antidepressants for mild depression.
That anxious knot in your stomach isn't a metaphor. Your gut talks to your brain more than your brain talks to your gut.
Food won't cure depression. But sugar crashes and skipped meals make every mental health condition harder.
Big changes are supposed to feel disorienting. The grief and excitement live in the same place.
Imposter syndrome isn't a sign you don't belong. It's the cost of caring about doing things well.
Codependency isn't about loving too much. It's about losing yourself in someone else's needs.
Emotional intelligence isn't about being calm. It's about naming what you feel before it runs you.
You'd never speak to a friend the way you speak to yourself. Self-compassion is just talking to yourself like someone you love.
If you're constantly questioning your own memory, that's not paranoia. That's the residue of being lied to systematically.
Overthinking feels like solving the problem. It's not. It's the problem dressed up as productivity.
